response.justification: Candidate is an accomplished full-stack software developer with strong web, mobile, and backend experience, but the role requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, performance architecture, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no evidence of GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture work, deep neural network optimization, or silicon-level performance analysis. The brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis on wave field synthesis are the closest adjacencies, and neither approaches the hardware/AI-accelerator depth required for a Principal Performance Architect.
strengths: Long, varied software engineering career with strong full-stack and systems breadth
Some low-level/performance exposure via DLang microservices handling millions of req/sec
Demonstrated ability to learn new domains and ship production systems independently
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator, or hardware-software co-design experience
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep learning training/inference optimization background
Educational and experience profile does not match Principal-level hardware performance architect requirements

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator / SoC modeling expertise
No GPU / SIMD / DNN optimization background
